FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. How do I give an item through the menu?

Type `/f1menu` in chat, find the desired item (you can use search), click on it, and select the quantity. The item will be given to your inventory.

---

 2. Why are some items displayed in red?

Items displayed in red are on the blacklist (vehicles). They are unavailable for giving through this menu.

---

 3. Can I modify the blacklist?

The blacklist is defined in the plugin's source code. To modify it, you need to edit the `F1ItemMenu.cs` file and recompile the plugin.

---

 4. How do I set up Discord notifications?

Enable `"Enable Discord Webhook"` in the configuration, specify the webhook URL, and configure the message format. Each time an item is given, a message will be sent to the specified channel.

---

 Troubleshooting

 The `/f1menu` command does not work

Cause: The player does not have the `f1itemmenu.f1` permission.

Solution: Grant the permission: `oxide.grant user nickname f1itemmenu.f1` or `oxide.grant group admin f1itemmenu.f1`.

---

 Notifications are not arriving in Discord

Cause: The webhook is disabled or the URL is incorrect.

Solution: Verify that `"Enable Discord Webhook"` is set to `true` and the webhook URL is correct. Make sure the server has internet access.

---

 The menu does not show all items

Cause: Items are distributed across pages (132 items per page).

Solution: Use the `<` and `>` navigation buttons to switch pages, or use the search field.

F1 Item Menu

 What does this plugin do?

F1 Item Menu provides administrators with a convenient visual menu for giving any in-game item. Instead of memorizing item shortnames, the administrator opens a graphical interface with icons of all items, selects the desired one, specifies the quantity, and receives it in their inventory. The plugin supports item searching, pagination, and Discord integration for logging item gives.

 Key Features

- Administrators see all game items in a convenient graphical menu
- Search items by name
- Pagination for navigating through a large list of items
- Quantity selection when giving items
- Item blacklist (vehicles are excluded by default)
- Discord notifications when items are given
- Configurable message format for Discord

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Oxide / uMod / Carbon | Required | Compatible with Oxide, uMod, and Carbon |

 Quick Start

1. Copy the `F1ItemMenu.cs` file to the `oxide/plugins/` folder.
2. Restart the server or run `oxide.reload F1ItemMenu`.
3. Grant permissions to administrators: `oxide.grant group admin f1itemmenu.f1`.
4. Type `/f1menu` in chat to open the menu.

F1 Item Menu Installation

 Step 1: Install the Plugin

1. Copy the `F1ItemMenu.cs` file to the `oxide/plugins/` folder.
2. The plugin will load automatically.
3. Check the console for errors.

The configuration file will be created automatically:
oxide/config/F1ItemMenu.json

 Step 2: Set Up Permissions

Grant the menu usage permission to administrators:

oxide.grant group admin f1itemmenu.f1

:::warning Important
Without the `f1itemmenu.f1` permission, the `/f1menu` command will not work. This permission is intended only for trusted administrators.
:::

 Step 3: Verify It Works

1. Join the server with the `f1itemmenu.f1` permission.
2. Type `/f1menu` in chat.
3. A visual menu with all game items will open.
4. Click on an item, select the quantity, and receive it.

 Step 4: Configure Discord (Optional)

To log item gives to Discord:

1. Create a webhook in your Discord channel.
2. Open `oxide/config/F1ItemMenu.json`.
3. Set `"Enable Discord Webhook": true`.
4. Paste the webhook URL into `"Webhook URL"`.

{
 "Enable Discord Webhook": true,
 "Webhook URL": "https://discord.com/api/webhooks/...",
 "Username": "Rust Server",
 "Avatar URL": "",
 "Discord Message Format": "Player {playerName} has taken {quantity} of {itemName}."
}

 Step 5: Reload the Plugin

oxide.reload F1ItemMenu

Commands and Permissions

 Chat Commands

The Genie plugin has no chat commands for regular players. Players use the lamp as a regular item in their inventory.

 Console Commands

| Command | Description | Who Can Use |
|---|---|---|
| `genie.give <steamid> <genieId>` | Give a lamp to the specified player | Administrators only |

 Usage Examples

Give a lamp with ID "default" to a player:
genie.give 76561198012345678 default

Give a lamp with ID "vip_lamp" to a player:
genie.give 76561198012345678 vip_lamp

:::warning Warning
If a non-existent lamp ID is specified or the player is not found, an error message will appear in the console.
:::

 Permissions

Permissions are configured individually for each lamp type in the configuration:

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| Permission | The permission required to use this lamp type |

If the `Permission` field is left empty (`""`), the lamp is available to all players.

 Permission Configuration Example

If the configuration specifies:
{
 "Permission": "genie.vip"
}

Grant the permission:
oxide.grant group vip genie.vip

 Gameplay Flow

1. The player receives a lamp (via admin command, loot container, or other plugins).
2. The lamp appears as a regular item with a custom skin.
3. Using the item opens the interface.
4. The player clicks "Rub the lamp" to start opening.
5. A progress animation with effects is displayed.
6. Upon completion, the player receives a random reward based on the configured chances.
7. The reward is granted automatically.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 1. How do I create a new lamp type?

In the configuration, add a new object with a unique `"ID"` to the `"Genies"` array. Copy the structure of an existing lamp and modify the parameters:

{
 "ID": "vip_lamp",
 "Enabled": true,
 "Permission": "genie.vip",
 "Lamp": {
 "DisplayName": "VIP Lamp",
 "ShortName": "xmas.present.small",
 "Skin": 2540200362
 },
 "Awards": [...]
}

---

 2. How do I give a lamp to a player?

Use the console command:
genie.give <steamid> <genieId>

For example:
genie.give 76561198012345678 default

---

 3. How do I configure reward drop chances?

Each reward has a `"Chance"` parameter from 0 to 100. The higher the value, the greater the drop chance:
- `70` -- high chance (70%)
- `50` -- medium chance (50%)
- `10` -- rare item (10%)
- `1` -- very rare item (1%)

---

 4. Can lamps drop from containers?

Yes. Lamps can be configured to drop from loot containers via the `"Drop"` parameter in the configuration. This allows lamps to appear in crates, barrels, and other containers.

---

 5. How do I restrict a lamp to VIP players only?

Specify a permission in the lamp settings:
{
 "Permission": "genie.vip"
}

Then grant the permission:
oxide.grant group vip genie.vip

Players without this permission will not be able to use this lamp type.

---

 6. The interface does not open when using a lamp. What should I do?

Possible causes:
1. ImageLibrary is not installed -- install the ImageLibrary plugin.
2. The lamp is disabled -- check that `"Enabled": true` in the configuration.
3. Missing permission -- if a Permission is specified, make sure the player has the corresponding permission.
4. Incorrect skin -- make sure the item skin matches the settings.

---

 7. How do I change the lamp opening time?

Change the `"Delay"` parameter in the lamp settings. The value is specified in seconds:
- `3` -- fast opening
- `5` -- standard (default)
- `10` -- slow, more dramatic

---

 8. Can I give commands as rewards?

Yes. Use

Genie

 What does this plugin do?

Genie adds a magic lamp (genie) system to your server. Players find special lamps as items, and when used, an opening animation plays and a random reward is granted. Each lamp has its own set of rewards with different drop chances.

 Key Features

- Lamps appear as regular in-game items with a custom skin
- Animated opening process with a progress bar
- Chance-based system for each reward
- Multiple genie types with different reward sets
- Rewards: items, commands, plugin currency
- Sound effects on opening, during progress, and on completion
- Configurable opening time and animation update frequency
- Lamps can drop from loot containers (optional)
- Control lamp stacking in inventory
- Permissions for each lamp type
- Integration with Notify for sending notifications
- Admin command to give lamps to players

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| ImageLibrary | Required | Needed for loading and displaying images |
| Notify | Optional | For sending graphical notifications |
| Oxide / uMod / Carbon | Required | Compatible with Oxide, uMod, and Carbon |

 Quick Start

1. Install the ImageLibrary plugin if it is not already installed.
2. Copy the `Genie.cs` file to your server's `oxide/plugins/` folder.
3. Restart the server or run the command `oxide.reload Genie`.
4. Give a lamp to a player with the command:
  genie.give <steamid> default
 5. The player will receive a lamp in their inventory. Using the item will open the interface with an animation.

Genie Setup

This guide will walk you through the installation process of the magic lamp plugin.

 Step 1: Install Dependencies

 ImageLibrary (required)

1. Download ImageLibrary from umod.org.
2. Place the `ImageLibrary.cs` file in the `oxide/plugins/` folder.
3. Make sure the plugin loads without errors.

:::warning Important
Without ImageLibrary, the Genie plugin will not work correctly. Players will see an error message.
:::

 Step 2: Install the Plugin

1. Copy the `Genie.cs` file to your server's `oxide/plugins/` folder.
2. The plugin will load automatically.
3. Check the console for errors.

The configuration file will be automatically created at:
oxide/config/Genie.json

 Step 3: Configure Permissions (if needed)

If a specific lamp type has a Permission specified, grant it to the appropriate players:

oxide.grant group vip genie.special

:::info Note
If the `Permission` field for a lamp is left empty, it will be available to all players without restrictions.
:::

 Step 4: Configure Lamps

Open the `oxide/config/Genie.json` file and configure the lamp settings:

- ID -- unique identifier for the lamp type
- Lamp -- item settings (ShortName, skin, name)
- Awards -- list of rewards with chances
- Delay -- opening time in seconds

 Step 5: Verify It Works

Give yourself a lamp with the command:
genie.give <your_steamid> default

Use the received item -- the interface with animation should open.

 Step 6: Reload the Plugin

After changing the configuration:

oxide.reload Genie

 Updating the Plugin

When updating:

1. Replace the `Genie.cs` file with the new version.
2. The plugin will automatically update the configuration.
3. Reload the plugin.

:::note Note
It is recommended to save a backup of the configuration before updating.
:::

Commands and Permissions

 Console Commands

| Command | Description | Example |

| `hsupply.give [nickname/steamid/all] [signal name] [amount]` | Give a custom supply signal to a player | `hsupply.give Player1 heal 3` |

 Command Parameters

- `[nickname/steamid/all]` -- player name, SteamID64, or `all` to give to everyone
- `[signal name]` -- supply signal type name (e.g., `heal`, `guns`, `explosive`)
- `[amount]` -- number of supply signals to give

 Examples

Give one medical supply signal to Player1.

hsupply.give 76561198012345678 guns 2
Give two weapon supply signals to a player by SteamID.

hsupply.give all explosive 1
Give one explosive supply signal to all players on the server.

 Permissions

The Helpful Supply plugin has no special permissions. The console command `hsupply.give` is executed from the server console or RCON.

:::info Note
Any player can use a custom supply signal if it is in their inventory. Access control is managed through supply signal distribution.
:::

FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. How do I create a new supply signal type?

Open the configuration and add a new object to the `"Supplies"` array. Specify a unique name, skin, and configure the airdrop contents. Reload the plugin.

---

 2. How do I give a supply signal to a player?

Use the console command: `hsupply.give nickname type amount`. For example: `hsupply.give Player1 heal 1`.

---

 3. Can I restrict who can open the airdrop?

Yes. In the loot settings of each type, there is an `"Access"` section. Set `"AllPlayers": false` and enable the desired options (`Teammates`, `Friends`, `Clanmates`). Then only the supply signal owner and the specified groups will be able to open the airdrop.

---

 4. How do I configure supply signals to drop from crates?

In the supply signal type configuration, enable `"Drop" > "Enabled": true` and add containers with drop chances to the `"Containers"` array.

---

 5. Can supply signals be used together with the Loottable plugin?

Yes. The plugin integrates with Loottable for advanced airdrop content management.

---

 Troubleshooting

 The airdrop arrives but is empty

Cause: Items in the `"Items"` section are configured incorrectly or drop chances are too low.

Solution: Check the item ShortNames and increase the drop chances. Make sure `"Amount"` has Min/Max values greater than zero.

---

 The `hsupply.give` command does not work

Cause: Incorrect command syntax or a non-existent supply signal type name was specified.

Solution: Check the syntax: `hsupply.give [nickname/steamid/all] [signal name] [amount]`. Make sure the signal name matches the `"Name"` field in the configuration.

---

 The supply signal looks like a regular Supply Signal

Cause: The skin failed to load or an invalid skin was specified.

Solution: Make sure the skin exists in the Steam Workshop. Reload the plugin to re-download skins.

Helpful Supply

 What does this plugin do?

Helpful Supply allows you to create custom supply signals with unique airdrop contents. Each supply signal type has its own skin, name, and loot list. For example, you can create a "Medical Supply Signal" with medical supplies or a "Weapons Supply Signal" with weapons. Custom supply signals can also drop from containers on the map.

 Key Features

- Create an unlimited number of supply signal types with unique skins
- Configure airdrop contents for each type (items, quantities, drop chances)
- Access control for airdrops (all players, teammates, friends, clan members)
- Drop speed configuration (fall speed, plane altitude)
- Custom supply signals drop from barrels and crates on the map
- Console command to give supply signals to players
- Integration with Friends, Clans, and Loottable plugins

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Oxide / uMod / Carbon | Required | Compatible with Oxide, uMod, and Carbon |
| Friends | Optional | Airdrop access for friends |
| Clans | Optional | Airdrop access for clan members |
| Loottable | Optional | Integration with loot tables |

 Quick Start

1. Copy the `HelpfulSupply.cs` file to the `oxide/plugins/` folder.
2. Restart the server or run `oxide.reload HelpfulSupply`.
3. Configure supply signal types in `oxide/config/HelpfulSupply.json`.
4. Give a supply signal to a player: `hsupply.give nickname heal 1`.

| `/marker` or `/hits` | Open the hit marker settings window | Configurable (available to all by default) |

:::info Note
Commands are configurable in the `"Commands"` parameter. Default: `["marker", "hits"]`.
:::

 Interface Settings

Through the interface, players can manage the following settings:

| Setting | Description |
|---|---|
| Text | Enable/disable numerical damage display |
| Icon | Enable/disable hit icon (with color change on headshot) |
| Health Line | Enable/disable enemy health bar |
| Buildings | Enable/disable building damage display |
| Font | Choose font for numerical damage |
| Font Size | Adjust font size |

 Permissions

| Permission | Description |
|---|---|
| Configurable | General permission for using markers (set in configuration, empty -- for everyone) |
| Font permissions | Individual permissions for each font |
| Mode permissions | Individual permissions for each mode (Text, Icon, Health Line, Buildings) |

:::tip Tip
If the `Permission` field is left empty, the feature will be available to all players.
:::

FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. How does a player customize markers?

Type `/marker` or `/hits` in chat. A window will open where you can enable/disable each marker type, choose a font, and set the text size.

---

 2. Markers do not appear when hitting NPCs

Check the `"Show damage to NPC"` parameter in the configuration. By default, it is enabled (`true`).

---

 3. Can I show damage to animals?

Yes. Set `"Show damage to animals": true` in the configuration.

---

 4. How do I change the marker display duration?

Change the `"Marker removal time"` parameter in the configuration. The value is specified in seconds.

---

 5. How do I restrict access to certain fonts?

For each font in the `"Fonts"` section, you can specify an access permission in the `"Permission"` field. The font will only be available to players with that permission.

---

 Troubleshooting

 Markers do not appear

Cause: ImageLibrary is not installed, or the player has disabled all modes in their settings.

Solution: Install ImageLibrary. Ask the player to open `/marker` and enable the desired modes.

---

 The health bar does not appear

Cause: The Health Line mode is disabled in the player's settings or in the configuration.

Solution: Enable the Health Line mode in the `"Buttons"` section of the configuration and make sure the player has activated it through the interface.

Hit Markers

 What does this plugin do?

Hit Markers adds visual hit markers to your server. Players see the numerical value of damage dealt, icon color changes on headshots, an enemy health bar, and building damage indicators. Each player can customize the markers to their liking: enable/disable individual elements, change the font and text size.

 Key Features

- Display numerical damage on hit (Text)
- Hit icon with color change on headshot (Icon)
- Enemy health bar above the hotbar slots (Health Line)
- Display damage to buildings (Buildings)
- Font, size, and style customization for each player
- Support for NPC and animal damage
- Integration with Notify for notifications
- Compatible with Oxide and Carbon

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| ImageLibrary | Required | Loading and displaying images |
| Notify / UINotify | Optional | Styled notifications |
| Oxide / uMod / Carbon | Required | Compatible with Oxide, uMod, and Carbon |

 Quick Start

1. Install ImageLibrary on the server.
2. Copy the `HitMarkers.cs` file to the `oxide/plugins/` folder.
3. Restart the server or run `oxide.reload HitMarkers`.
4. Type `/marker` or `/hits` in chat to open the marker settings.

Hit Markers Setup

 Step 1: Install Dependencies

 ImageLibrary (required)

 Step 2: Install the Plugin

2. The plugin will load automatically.

oxide/config/HitMarkers.json

 Step 3: Verify It Works

2. Type `/marker` in chat.
3. The marker settings window will open.

Frequently Asked Questions -- Kits

 1. How do I create a new kit?

Join the game with administrator privileges and type the `/editkit` command. The visual editor will open, where you can create a new kit, add items, configure cooldown, price, and access permissions. All changes are saved automatically.

 2. How do I make a kit available only to VIP players?

When creating a kit in the editor, specify an access permission, for example `kits.vip`. Then grant this permission to the desired group:
oxide.grant group vip kits.vip

 3. How do I set up a paid kit?

In the kit editor, specify the kit's price. The currency is configured in the plugin configuration -- you can use Economics, ServerRewards, or in-game items (scrap, wood, etc.).

 4. Can I give kits to offline players?

Yes, use the `kits.givekit <steamid> <name> [amount]` command. This will grant kit uses to the player, and they can claim it from the kit menu on their next login.

 5. How do I set up an auto-kit on server join?

Add the kit name to the auto-kits list in the configuration:
"Auto Kits": ["autokit", "autokit_vip", "autokit_premium"]
Kits are checked from bottom to top -- the player receives the first kit they have permission for.

 6. Is kit data reset on wipe?

By default, data is not reset. To enable automatic reset, set in the configuration:
"Automatic wipe on wipe": true
You can also reset data manually with the `kits.reset data all` command.

 7. How do I link kits to NPCs on the map?

In the configuration, add the NPC ID and kit list:
"NPC Kits": {
 "YOUR_NPC_ID": {
 "Description": "Free kits",
 "Kits": ["starter", "daily"]
 }
}
When interacting with the NPC, the player will see the linked kits.

 8. How do I allow claiming certain kits during raid block?

Add kit names to the NoEscape whitelist in the configuration:
"Whitelist for NoEscape": ["medical_kit", "emergency_kit"]

 9. How do I change the interface appearance?

Run the `kits.template` command from the server console to view availa

Kits

 What does this plugin do?

Kits allows your server to provide players with pre-configured item sets (kits). Administrators create kits with specific contents, set cooldowns, permission restrictions, and prices. Players can claim kits through a convenient in-game interface, chat commands, or from NPC vendors.

 Key Features

- Create an unlimited number of kits with any items
- Configurable cooldowns and usage limits for each kit
- Permission system -- different kits for different player groups
- Purchase kits with in-game currency (Economics, ServerRewards, or items)
- Auto-kits on server join (with player opt-out option)
- Link kits to NPCs on the map
- Fully customizable interface (fullscreen and embedded in menu)
- Kit category support
- Integration with CopyPaste for building distribution
- Block claiming during raid or combat (NoEscape)
- Automatic kit data wipe on server wipe
- Discord logging
- In-game kit editing (visual editor)
- Oxide and Carbon support

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required? | Description |
|---|---|---|
| ImageLibrary | Yes | Required for displaying images in the interface |
| ServerPanel | No | Integration with the server menu panel |
| CopyPaste | No | For distributing buildings through kits |
| Notify / UINotify | No | Styled notifications for players |
| NoEscape | No | Block kits during raid/combat |

 Quick Start

1. Install the ImageLibrary plugin (required)
2. Place the plugin file in the `oxide/plugins` folder (or `carbon/plugins`)
3. Restart the server or run `o.reload Kits`
4. Open the configuration file and set up commands, economy, and interface
5. Use the `/editkit` command in-game to create kits through the visual editor
6. Grant the necessary permissions to players or groups

 Permissions

| Permission | Description |
|---|---|
| `Kits.admin` | Full access to kit management and administrative commands |
| `Kits.dlc` | Bypass DLC item restrictions |
| `Kits.bypasscooldown` | Bypass cooldown when claiming kits |
| `

Commands and Permissions

 Chat Commands

:::tip Tip
Commands are configurable in the `Open UI Commands` config option. Default: `["link"]`.
:::

 Usage Examples

Open the UI:
/link

Check link status:
/link status

Remove the link:
/link remove

 Console Commands (internal)

These commands are used by the Discord bot (LinkBot) to interact with the plugin. They aren't meant for manual use:

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `LinkBot_BotOnline` | Bot reports it's connected |
| `LinkBot_UpdatePlayer` | Update player link data |
| `LinkBot_RemovePlayer` | Remove a player's link |

:::warning Warning
These commands are used exclusively by the Discord bot via RCON. Don't run them manually.
:::

 Permissions

| Permission | Description | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `linkingsystem.use` | Access to the linking system | Not granted |

 Setting Up Permissions

oxide.grant group default linkingsystem.use

 Linking Process

 For Players

1. Type `/link` in chat on the server.
2. Click "CONNECT DISCORD" in the UI.
3. Generate a unique code.
4. Copy the code (CTRL+C).
5. Go to the `#verify` Discord channel on your server.
6. Paste the code in chat or use the Discord bot.
7. Go back to the game and click "CHECK CONNECTION STATUS".
8. If successful, the status changes to "Account linked".

 Unlinking

1. Type `/link` to open the UI.
2. Click "DISCONNECT ACCOUNT".
3. Or use the `/link remove` command.

 Link Statuses

| Status | Description |
|---|---|
| Not Linked | Account isn't linked |
| Wait Confirm | Code generated, waiting for confirmation |
| Linked | Account successfully linked |

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 1. The bot won't connect. What do I do?

Check the following:
1. The Discord bot (LinkBot) is configured and running.
2. The bot has access to your server's RCON.
3. RCON is enabled in your server settings.
4. There are no connection errors in the server console.

When the bot connects successfully, you'll see: `LinkBot is connected`.

---

 2. How do I change the group for linked players?

Change this value in the config:
"What group for linked players": "discord_vip"

Create the group and set up its privileges:
oxide.group add discord_vip

---

 3. The code is generated but linking doesn't confirm. Why?

Possible reasons:
1. Bot isn't active -- check the bot status with `/link status`.
2. Wrong channel -- the player is entering the code in the wrong Discord channel.
3. Code expired -- generate a new code.
4. RCON error -- check the bot's RCON connection.

---

 4. How often can a new code be generated?

The default cooldown is 60 seconds. Change it in the config:
"Code generation delay (sec.)": 30

---

 5. Can I disable the UI and just use commands?

Yes. Set this in the config:
"Use UI ?": false

Players will then manage linking through commands:
- `/link status` -- check status
- `/link code` -- get a code
- `/link remove` -- remove the link

---

 6. How do I set up bonuses for linking?

Bonuses are configured through an Oxide group. Create the group:
oxide.group add discord

Then set up whatever privileges you want for that group through other plugins. The `"Rewards UI elements"` section only controls what's displayed in the UI.

---

 7. A player wants to link a different Discord account. How?

1. First unlink the current account: `/link remove` or through the UI.
2. Then generate a new code and link the other account.

---

 8. How do I show an item as a reward in the UI?

Use the `"Element itemId"` parameter with the item ID:

 "Element itemId": -852563019,

Linking System

 What does this plugin do?

Linking System lets players link their Steam account to Discord. A player generates a code in-game, enters it in the Discord bot, and the link is confirmed. Players can receive rewards for linking: extra groups, bonuses, and items.

 Key Features

- Link Steam accounts to Discord via code generation
- Automatically add players to a group after linking
- Clean UI showing link status
- Display Discord account info (name, avatar)
- Configurable rewards for linking (shown in the UI)
- Option to unlink accounts
- Cooldown on generating new codes
- Chat commands for managing links
- Discord bot integration via RCON commands
- Integration with ServerPanel, Notify
- Multiple UI templates (V1, V2)

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| ImageLibrary | Required | For images in the UI |
| Discord bot (LinkBot) | Required | Handles linking on the Discord side |
| Notify | Optional | For graphical notifications |
| ServerPanel | Optional | For server menu integration |
| Oxide / uMod / Carbon | Required | Compatible with Oxide, uMod, and Carbon |

 Quick Start

1. Install the ImageLibrary plugin.
2. Copy `LinkingSystem.cs` to the `oxide/plugins/` folder.
3. Set up the Discord bot (LinkBot) for your server.
4. Restart the server or run `oxide.reload LinkingSystem`.
5. Grant the permission:
  oxide.grant group default linkingsystem.use
 6. Join the server and type `/link` to open the UI.

Commands and Permissions

 Console Commands

| Command | Description | Permission |
|---|---|---|
| `barrelevent.start` | Start the event manually | `MultiEventsBarrelEvent.start` |
| `barrelevent.stop` | Stop the current event | `MultiEventsBarrelEvent.stop` |

:::info

 Permissions

| Permission | Description |
|---|---|
| `MultiEventsBarrelEvent.start` | Permission to start the event |
| `MultiEventsBarrelEvent.stop` | Permission to stop the event |

 Assigning Permissions

oxide.grant group admin MultiEventsBarrelEvent.start
oxide.grant group admin MultiEventsBarrelEvent.stop

 UI Controls

Players can hide the event widget by clicking the close button on the notification panel. The widget will automatically reappear on the next event start.

Barrel Event Configuration

The config file is created automatically when the plugin first loads.

 Main Settings

 Work with Notify?

Enables or disables integration with Notify / UINotify for sending notifications. If disabled, notifications go to chat instead.

"Work with Notify?": true

 Event Duration

Event duration in seconds. Default is 600 seconds (10 minutes).

"Event Duration": 600

 Barrel Points

The `Barrels point` section defines how many points a player gets for destroying each barrel type. The key is the barrel's internal name, the value is the point amount.

"Barrels point": {
 "loot-barrel-1": 1,
 "loot-barrel-2": 2,
 "loot_barrel_1": 1,
 "loot_barrel_2": 2,
 "oil_barrel": 3
}

You can add, remove, or change barrel types and their point values.

 Award Settings

Rewards are configured per placement.

 Reward Structure

Each placement contains:

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| Inventory Awards | Cell indices in the Multi Events inventory |
| Amount (min/max) | Number of rewards to give |
| Awards | List of possible rewards |

 Reward Types

Each reward can be one of these types:

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| Item | In-game item (ShortName, amount, skin) |

Frequently Asked Questions

 General

 Do I need Multi Events for this mode to work?

Yes. Multi Events is a required dependency. Barrel Event is an expansion mode that can't run on its own.

 What barrel types are supported?

All standard Rust barrels are supported by default: regular loot barrels (loot-barrel) and oil barrels (oil_barrel). You can add any other barrel types in the config.

 How long does the event last?

10 minutes (600 seconds) by default. You can change this in the config with the `Event Duration` parameter.

 Configuration

 How do I change barrel points?

Edit the `Barrels point` section in the config. The key is the barrel's short name, the value is the point count. Reload the plugin after making changes.

 How do I set up rewards?

In the `Awards Settings` section, you can configure rewards for each placement. Items, console commands, kits, and third-party plugin calls are all supported.

 Can I add more prize positions?

Yes. Add extra entries in the `Awards Settings` section with position numbers (3, 4, 5, etc.), each with its own reward set.

 Problems

 Points aren't awarded for destroying barrels

Make sure the barrel short names in the config match the actual object names on the map. Also check that the event is actually running.

 UI widget doesn't show up

Check that ImageLibrary is installed and working. Also make sure the player hasn't hidden the widget manually.

 Event ends but rewards aren't given

Make sure the reward settings in the config are correct: valid ShortName values, amounts, and drop chances.

Barrel Event

 What does this mode do?

Barrel Event is an event mode for Multi Events where players compete for points by smashing barrels across the map. Each barrel type awards a different number of points -- rarer barrels give more. The player with the most points when the timer runs out wins.

 Key Features

- Configurable point system for each barrel type (regular, oil, etc.)
- Flexible reward system for multiple leaderboard positions
- Notify and UINotify support for notifications
- Built-in UI widget with leaderboard and countdown
- Rewards via items, commands, kits, and third-party plugins
- Integration with Multi Events Inventory

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required? |
|---|---|
| Multi Events | Yes |
| ImageLibrary | Yes |
| Multi Events Inventory | Recommended |
| Multi Events Leaderboard | Recommended |
| Notify / UINotify | Optional |

 Quick Start

1. Install Multi Events on the server
2. Place the Barrel Event file in the `oxide/plugins` folder
3. Restart the server or load the plugin
4. The config file gets created automatically
5. Configure barrel points and rewards in the config
6. The event is ready to launch through Multi Events or manually

 How the Event Works

1. The event starts automatically through Multi Events or manually by an admin
2. All players on the server see a UI notification with the description and timer
3. Players start destroying barrels across the map, earning points
4. The leaderboard updates in real time with current results
5. When the timer expires, the event ends
6. Winners receive rewards based on their placement

FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. Which NPC bots count in the event?

By default, Bradley-related scientists count: `scientistnpc_bradley` (2 points) and `scientistnpc_bradley_heavy` (3 points). You can add any other NPC type in the config by specifying their ShortPrefabName and point value.

---

 2. How do I add new bot types?

Open the config and add entries to the `"Bots point"` section:

"Bots point": {
 "scientistnpc_bradley": 2.0,
 "scientistnpc_bradley_heavy": 3.0,
 "scientistnpc_oilrig": 5.0,
 "scientistnpc_junkpile_pistol": 1.0
}

---

 3. Can I set up rewards for multiple positions?

Yes. In the `"Player Awards (top - award)"` section, add entries for each position: `"1"` for first place, `"2"` for second, and so on.

---

 4. What happens if time runs out and nobody scored points?

The event ends with no winner. No rewards are given.

---

 5. Can the event run automatically on a schedule?

Yes. The event registers with MultiEvents and can be included in automatic scheduling through the core MultiEvents settings.

---

 Event won't start

Symptoms: The `botpurge.start` command doesn't launch the event.

Cause: MultiEvents isn't installed or isn't loaded.

Fix: Make sure MultiEvents is installed and running. Check the console for errors when loading the plugin.

---

 Points aren't awarded for killing NPCs

Symptoms: Players kill bots, but points don't register.

Fix: Check the ShortPrefabName of the killed NPC and add it to the `"Bots point"` section. Also make sure the event is actually running.

---

 Event panel doesn't show up

Cause: Could be an ImageLibrary issue, or the player hid the panel.

FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. Which weapons count in the event?

Only kills made with arrows (Arrow damage type). This includes the Hunting Bow, Compound Bow, and Crossbow.

---

 2. Do animal kills count?

Yes. Points are awarded for kills on both players (BasePlayer) and animals (BaseAnimalNPC) made with arrows.

---

 3. How many points per kill?

Each arrow kill gives 1 point, regardless of target type.

---

 4. Do fire arrow kills count?

No. If the final hit comes from the fire of a fire arrow rather than the arrow itself, the kill won't count because the damage type won't be Arrow.

---

 5. Can I set different points for players vs. animals?

In the current version, points are fixed at 1 per arrow kill.

---

 Troubleshooting

 Points aren't being awarded

Symptoms: A player kills things with a bow but points don't register.

Cause: The damage type isn't Arrow (e.g., fire from a fire arrow), or the event isn't active.

Fix: Make sure the event is running. Check that you're using a regular bow or crossbow with regular arrows.

---

 Event doesn't show in MultiEvents

Symptoms: The event doesn't appear in the available events list.

Cause: MultiEvents wasn't loaded, or was loaded after Bow Master.

Fix: Reload the plugin: `oxide.reload MultiEventsBowMaster`. It'll auto-register with MultiEvents.

---

 Event panel doesn't display

Symptoms: The event started but the UI panel isn't visible.

Cause: ImageLibrary issue or the player hid the panel.

Fix: Make sure ImageLibrary is installed. The panel will reappear on the next event start.

 Tool Multipliers

The `Tools Multiplier` section lets you set point multipliers based on the tool being used.

"Tools Multiplier": {
 "jackhammer": 0.5,
 "rock": 1.5
}

- Key -- tool short name
- Value -- point multiplier (0.5 = half points, 1.5 = 1.5x points, 1.0 = no change)

Tools not listed default to `1.0`.

:::tip Tip
Lower the multiplier for powerful tools (like `jackhammer`) and raise it for weak ones (like `rock`) to keep the event balanced.
:::

 Item Multipliers

The `Items Multiplier` section lets you set multipliers for specific resource types, including skin variants.

"Items Multiplier": {
 "metal.ore": {
 "Skin": 0,
 "Multiplier": 1.0
 },
 "sulfur.ore": {
 "Skin": 0,
 "Multiplier": 1.0
 }
}

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| Skin | Item skin ID (0 = default) |
| Multiplier | Point multiplier for this item |

 Permission Multipliers

The `Permissions Multiplier` section lets you set point multipliers for players with specific permissions. All listed permissions are registered automatically on plugin load.

"Permissions Multiplier": {
 "MultiEventsCollectionResources.default": 1.0,
 "MultiEventsCollectionResources.vip": 2.0
}

- Key -- permission name
- Value -- point multiplier for players with that permission

If a player has multiple permissions from the list, the highest multiplier applies.

:::info How Multipliers Work
Final points are calculated as:

`points = base_amount  tool_multiplier  item_multiplier * permission_multiplier`

All three multipliers stack multiplicatively.
:::

 Award Settings

The `Awards Settings` section configures winner rewards.

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Put the reward in the MultiEv

Frequently Asked Questions

 General

 What types of gathering count?

All resource gathering counts: chopping trees, mining rocks and ore, picking plants, and picking up items from the ground. The system tracks all standard resource collection methods in the game.

 How does the multiplier system work?

Final points are calculated by applying three multipliers in sequence:
1. Tool multiplier -- depends on which tool the player is using
2. Item multiplier -- depends on the type of resource being gathered
3. Permission multiplier -- depends on the player's privileges

For example, if base points = 10, tool multiplier = 0.5, item multiplier = 1, permission multiplier = 2, then final points = 10  0.5  1 * 2 = 10.

 Do players need to register?

No. All players on the server automatically participate.

 Configuration

 How do I balance the event for high-rate servers?

Lower the multipliers for powerful tools (e.g., `jackhammer: 0.3`) and raise them for weak ones (`rock: 2.0`). You can also lower multipliers for commonly gathered resources.

 How do I set up VIP multipliers?

Add the permissions and multipliers you want in the `Permissions Multiplier` config section, then grant those permissions to the right groups through the server's permission system.

 Can I set multipliers for items with a specific skin?

Yes. Each item in the `Items Multiplier` section has a `Skin` parameter. Set the skin ID for an exact match. A value of `0` means the standard item with no skin.

 Technical

 Is the plugin compatible with Carbon?

Yes. It works with both Oxide and Carbon.

 What happens if a player has multiple permission multipliers?

The highest multiplier from all the player's permissions is used. Permission multipliers don't stack additively.

 How does the edit permission work?

Players with the edit permission can change event settings through the in-game UI without touching the config file on the server.

 Step-by-Step Installation

 Updating

When updating, just replace the file in the plugins folder. The config updates automatically and keeps your settings.

 Load Order

The plugin auto-registers with Multi Events on load. If Multi Events loads later, registration happens automatically when it becomes available.

FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. Which corn counts?

All collectible objects (CollectibleEntity) with "corn" in their prefab name. This means standard wild corn growing on the map.

---

 2. Does corn from planters count?

No. The plugin only tracks corn picked up through the OnCollectiblePickup event, which applies to wild corn on the map, not corn grown in planters.

---

 3. How many points per ear?

Each ear of corn gives 1 point.

---

 4. What happens when the event ends?

The winner is the player with the most points. They and other top finishers (if configured) receive rewards.

---

 5. Can I run the event on a map with no corn?

Technically yes, but it'd be pointless since nobody can score. Only use this mode on maps with enough wild corn.

---

 Troubleshooting

 Points aren't awarded for collecting corn

Symptoms: A player collects corn but points don't register.

Cause: The event isn't active, or the player is collecting something other than corn.

Fix: Make sure the event is running with `corncollector.start`. Check that it's actually corn being collected.

---

 Event doesn't register in MultiEvents

Symptoms: The event doesn't appear in the MultiEvents event list.

Cause: MultiEvents wasn't loaded or was loaded after Corn Collector.

Fix: Reload the plugin: `oxide.reload MultiEventsCornCollector`.

---

 No corn on the map

Symptoms: The event is running but players can't find corn.

Cause: The map doesn't have enough wild corn, or resources haven't respawned yet.

Fix: Wait for resources to respawn, or use a map with more vegetation.

Corn Collector

 Description

Corn Collector is an expansion mode for MultiEvents where players compete to pick corn. Each ear of corn you collect earns a point. The player who collects the most corn by the end of the event wins.

 Key Features

- Competitive corn collection mode
- Points for every ear of corn collected
- Real-time leaderboard showing top players
- Configurable rewards by ranking position
- Integration with MultiEventsInventory for reward delivery
- Integration with MultiEventsLeaderboard for global stats
- Notify / UINotify support
- Configurable event duration
- UI panel with countdown timer and current scores

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| MultiEvents | Required | Core events plugin |
| ImageLibrary | Required | Image loading and display |
| MultiEventsInventory | Optional | Event inventory reward delivery |
| MultiEventsLeaderboard | Optional | Global leaderboard tracking |
| Notify / UINotify | Optional | Visual player notifications |

 Quick Start

1. Make sure MultiEvents and ImageLibrary are installed and running.
2. Copy `MultiEventsCornCollector.cs` to your server's `oxide/plugins/` folder.
3. The plugin automatically registers with MultiEvents.
4. Start the event through MultiEvents or with:
  corncollector.start
 5. Collect corn on the map to earn points.

Setup and Configuration

 ImageLibrary (required)

Corn Collector won't work without both MultiEvents and ImageLibrary.

 Step 2: Install the Plugin

 Commands

 Admin Commands

:::info Note
The event can also start automatically through MultiEvents scheduling.
:::

 Setting Up Permissions

oxide.grant group admin MultiEventsFoundationDrop.start
oxide.grant group admin MultiEventsFoundationDrop.stop

 Player Commands

Players join the event through the vote button in the UI during the registration phase. There are no special chat commands.

 Event Phases

1. Voting -- players click a button to join. Duration set by the `Voting` parameter.
2. Waiting -- arena setup and player teleportation. Duration set by the `Waiting` parameter.
3. Game -- foundations start crumbling, players must survive.
4. Final battle -- when one foundation remains, radiation turns on and weapons are given out.
5. End -- last player standing wins.

 Restrictions During the Event

While the event is active, participants can't:
- Use commands not listed in `"Allowed commands on event"`
- Teleport (via NTeleportation)
- Leave the zone (via NoEscape, if enabled)

Configuration

| Work with NoEscape? | `false` | Use the NoEscape plugin to block zone exit |
| Allowed commands on event | `["cmd1", "cmd2", "cmd3"]` | Commands allowed during the event |

`"Block Degrade Settings"` section:

|---|---|---|

| Distance | `0.5` | Distance to check if a player is on a block |

|---|---|---|

FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. How does the event work?

Players register through voting, then get teleported to a foundation arena (NxN grid). Foundations start crumbling one by one. Players have to jump between remaining blocks to avoid falling. Last one standing wins.

---

 2. What happens in the final phase?

When one foundation remains, the final battle begins: radiation turns on (if configured), and each surviving player gets a weapon (salvaged sword by default) or a kit. Players fight to the death.

---

 3. What's the minimum player count?

2 players by default. You can change it in the config (`"Minimal players to start event"`), but it can't go below 2.

---

 4. Can multiple players win?

Yes. If `"Give out a reward to all players who stayed on 1 block"` is enabled, all players left on the last foundation get a reward. The `"Maximum number of winners"` parameter caps how many can win.

---

 5. Which commands can be used during the event?

All commands are blocked during the event except those listed in `"Allowed commands on event"`. The defaults are `cmd1`, `cmd2`, `cmd3` -- replace them with whatever you need.

---

 6. What if a player disconnects during the event?

The player automatically leaves the event. If remaining players drop below the minimum, the event may end.

---

 Troubleshooting

 Event gets canceled due to not enough players

Symptoms: Message appears saying the event was canceled due to insufficient players.

Cause: Not enough players signed up during voting.

Fix: Lower the `"Minimal players to start event"` value or increase the voting time (`"Voting"`) to give players more time to register.

---

 Foundations crumble too fast or too slow

Symptoms: The game ends too quickly or drags on.

Cause: The destruction delay is set too low or too high.

Fix: Change the `"Delay"` value in `"Block Degrade Settings"`. Higher = slower destruction, lower = faster.

---

 Players can't join

Symptoms: The vote button doesn't work

Foundation Drop

 Description

Foundation Drop is an expansion mode for MultiEvents -- a survival minigame. Players spawn on a platform made of foundations that start crumbling one by one. You have to jump between the remaining foundations to avoid falling. The last player standing wins. When only one foundation remains, a final phase kicks in with radiation and weapons.

 Key Features

- Survival minigame with crumbling foundations
- Configurable arena size (NxN grid)
- Voting phase for player registration
- Final battle with radiation and weapons on the last foundation
- Configurable kit or item for the final phase
- Support for multiple winners
- Command blocking during the event
- Integration with NoEscape, NTeleportation, and Vanish
- Integration with MultiEventsInventory for reward delivery
- Integration with MultiEventsLeaderboard for global stats
- Notify / UINotify support
- Configurable minimum player count

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| MultiEvents | Required | Core events plugin |
| ImageLibrary | Required | Image loading and display |
| MultiEventsInventory | Optional | Event inventory reward delivery |
| MultiEventsLeaderboard | Optional | Global leaderboard |
| Kits | Optional | For giving kits in the final phase |
| Vanish | Optional | For detecting invisible players |
| NTeleportation | Optional | For blocking teleportation during the event |
| NoEscape | Optional | For blocking zone exit during the event |
| Notify / UINotify | Optional | Visual notifications |

 Quick Start

1. Make sure MultiEvents and ImageLibrary are installed and running.
2. Copy `MultiEventsFoundationDrop.cs` to your server's `oxide/plugins/` folder.
3. The plugin automatically registers with MultiEvents.
4. Configure minimum player count and arena size in the config.
5. Start the event through MultiEvents or with:
  foundationdrop.start

FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. How is Gold Rush different from other MultiEvents modes?

Unlike competitive modes (Bot Purge, Bow Master, etc.), Gold Rush is a bonus mode. All players get extra items when mining ore and chopping trees -- there's no competition for points. There's no leaderboard -- everyone participates at the same time.

---

 2. Which resources give bonuses?

Bonuses drop when gathering two resource types:
- Ore -- stone, sulfur ore, metal ore, etc.
- Trees -- all tree types

Each type has its own drop table.

---

 3. How do VIP multipliers work?

Players with the `MultiEventsGoldRush.vip` permission (or another configured one) get increased drop chances. For example, a 2.0 multiplier doubles each item's chance. If an item has a 20% chance, it becomes 40% for VIP players.

---

 4. Can the chance go above 100%?

If the base chance multiplied by the VIP multiplier exceeds 100%, the item drops guaranteed on every hit.

---

 5. Can I add multiple VIP tiers?

Yes. Add new permissions in the `"Permissions Multiplier chane"` section:
{
 "MultiEventsGoldRush.vip": 2.0,
 "MultiEventsGoldRush.premium": 3.0,
 "MultiEventsGoldRush.elite": 5.0
}
Each permission needs to be registered through Oxide. If a player has multiple permissions, the highest multiplier is used.

---

 Troubleshooting

 Bonus items don't drop

Symptoms: A player mines resources but doesn't get extra items.

Cause: The event isn't active, or the drop chances are too low.

Fix: Make sure the event is running with `goldrush.start`. Check drop chances in the config -- set them high (80-100%) for testing.

---

 VIP multiplier doesn't work

Symptoms: A VIP player doesn't get boosted chances.

Cause: The permission isn't granted or registered.

Fix: Make sure the permission is granted: `oxide.grant group vip MultiEventsGoldRush.vip`. The plugin auto-registers permissions from the config on load.

---

 Event panel doesn't show

Symptoms: The event is active but the UI panel i

Gold Rush

 Description

Gold Rush is an expansion mode for MultiEvents where mining ore and chopping trees gives you a chance to get bonus items. Every hit on a resource node (ore or tree) rolls for a bonus item from a configurable drop table. Ore and trees have separate reward tables with different drop rates.

 Key Features

- Bonus items from mining ore and chopping trees
- Separate drop tables for ore and trees
- Configurable drop chance per item
- Chance multiplier system based on permissions (VIP)
- Notify / UINotify support
- Configurable event duration
- UI panel with countdown timer and event description

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| MultiEvents | Required | Core events plugin |
| ImageLibrary | Required | Image loading and display |
| Notify / UINotify | Optional | Visual player notifications |

 Quick Start

1. Make sure MultiEvents and ImageLibrary are installed and running.
2. Copy `MultiEventsGoldRush.cs` to your server's `oxide/plugins/` folder.
3. The plugin automatically registers with MultiEvents.
4. Configure the ore and tree drop tables in the config.
5. Start the event through MultiEvents or with:
  goldrush.start
 6. Mine ore or chop trees to get bonus items.

- ImageLibrary plugin installed (required)

 Step 1: Install Dependencies

 MultiEvents (required)

 ImageLibrary (required)

2. Place `ImageLibrary.cs` in the `oxide/plugins/` folder.

:::warning Important
Gold Rush won't work without both MultiEvents and ImageLibrary.

 Step 2: Install the Plugin

 Step 3: Set Up Permissions

For the VIP chance multiplier (optional):

- Ore items -- items and drop chances for mining ore

 Player Commands

Regular players don't have special commands. Participation is automatic -- just attract the helicopter's attention during the active event.

 Scoring Mechanics

Points are awarded based on the helicopter AI's state toward the player:

| Helicopter State | Points |
|---|---|
| Orbit (ORBIT) -- helicopter circles around the player | 1 point |
| Attack (STRAFE) -- helicopter attacks the player | 2 points |

:::warning Warning
Attracting the helicopter is dangerous -- it will shoot at you! Players need to balance scoring points with staying alive.
:::

 Event End Conditions

The event ends when:
- The event timer expires
- The helicopter is destroyed (health reaches 0)
- An admin stops the event with `helicopterpet.stop`

FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. How do I earn points?

Points are awarded automatically when the helicopter pays attention to you. If it circles around you (orbit mode), you get 1 point. If it attacks you (strafe mode), you get 2 points.

---

 2. Do I need to shoot the helicopter?

Shooting the helicopter can attract its attention and make it attack you, which gives more points (2 instead of 1). But be careful -- it shoots back!

---

 3. What happens if the helicopter is destroyed?

If the helicopter's health drops to zero, the event ends early. Increase the `"Helicopter Health"` config value to prevent this.

---

 4. Where does the helicopter spawn?

It spawns at the position set in `"Heli Init Position"`. If left at the default `(0, 0, 0)`, it spawns at the map center.

---

 5. Can players die from the helicopter?

Yes. The helicopter behaves like a regular patrol helicopter and will fire at players it's targeting. That's part of the event -- players have to balance scoring with survival.

---

 6. Do points carry over after death?

Yes. Points earned before death are kept. A player can keep scoring after respawning.

---

 Troubleshooting

 Helicopter doesn't spawn

Symptoms: The event started but no helicopter appears.

Cause: Invalid spawn position or initialization error.

Fix: Check `"Heli Init Position"` in the config. Make sure the coordinates are within the map bounds. Check the console for errors.

---

 Helicopter gets destroyed too fast

Symptoms: The event ends early because the helicopter is destroyed.

Cause: Helicopter health is too low.

Fix: Increase `"Helicopter Health"` in the config. 10000+ is recommended for servers with experienced players.

---

 Points aren't awarded

Symptoms: The helicopter is flying around but points aren't registering.

Cause: The helicopter hasn't locked onto the player as a target.

Fix: Attract the helicopter's attention -- shoot at it, stand in the open. Points only count when the helicopter AI i

Helicopter Pet

 Description

Helicopter Pet is an expansion mode for MultiEvents where players compete for the patrol helicopter's attention. The plugin spawns a special helicopter, and players earn points when it orbits or attacks them. The more attention the helicopter gives you, the more points you get. Top scorer at the end wins.

 Key Features

- Unique patrol helicopter mode
- Two point types: orbit (1 point) and attack (2 points)
- Configurable helicopter health
- Configurable spawn position and flight height
- Real-time leaderboard
- Configurable rewards by ranking position
- Integration with MultiEventsInventory and MultiEventsLeaderboard
- Notify / UINotify support
- Configurable event duration
- UI panel with timer and current scores

 Requirements

| Dependency | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| MultiEvents | Required | Core events plugin |
| ImageLibrary | Required | Image loading and display |
| MultiEventsInventory | Optional | Event inventory reward delivery |
| MultiEventsLeaderboard | Optional | Global leaderboard tracking |
| Notify / UINotify | Optional | Visual player notifications |

 Quick Start

1. Make sure MultiEvents and ImageLibrary are installed and running.
2. Copy `MultiEventsHelicopterPet.cs` to your server's `oxide/plugins/` folder.
3. The plugin automatically registers with MultiEvents.
4. Configure helicopter health and spawn position in the config.
5. Start the event through MultiEvents or with:
  helicopterpet.start
 6. Get the helicopter's attention to score points!

Setup and Configuration

This guide walks you through installing the Helicopter Pet mode for MultiEvents.

 System Requirements

- Rust server with Oxide / uMod / Carbon
- MultiEvents plugin installed (required)
- ImageLibrary plugin installed (required)

 Step 1: Install Dependencies

 MultiEvents (required)

1. Install MultiEvents following its documentation.
2. Make sure the plugin loads without errors.

 ImageLibrary (required)

1. Download ImageLibrary from umod.org.
2. Place `ImageLibrary.cs` in the `oxide/plugins/` folder.

:::warning Important
Helicopter Pet won't work without both MultiEvents and ImageLibrary.
:::

 Step 2: Install the Plugin

1. Copy `MultiEventsHelicopterPet.cs` to your server's `oxide/plugins/` folder.
2. The plugin loads automatically and registers with MultiEvents.
3. Check the console for errors.

Config file created automatically:
oxide/config/MultiEventsHelicopterPet.json

 Step 3: Set Up Permissions

oxide.grant group admin MultiEventsHelicopterPet.start
oxide.grant group admin MultiEventsHelicopterPet.stop

 Step 4: Configure the Helicopter

Set up these key helicopter parameters in the config:

- Heli Init Position -- helicopter spawn position (X, Y, Z coordinates)
- Heli Height above ground -- flight height above ground
- Helicopter Health -- helicopter health

:::tip Tip
If the spawn position is left at `0, 0, 0`, the helicopter spawns at the map center. Setting a position near active player bases works best.
:::

 Step 5: Test It

helicopterpet.start

A patrol helicopter spawns on the map and players see the event panel. Attract the helicopter's attention to score points.

 Step 6: Reload the Plugin

oxide.reload MultiEventsHelicopterPet

:::warning Warning
The Helicopter Pet event is dangerous -- the helicopter will shoot at players! Make sure players know this. Set the helicopter health high enough that it won't get destroyed before the event ends.
:::

FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. Which hemp counts?

All collectible objects (CollectibleEntity) with "hemp" in their prefab name. This means wild hemp growing on the map.

---

 2. Does hemp from planters count?

No. The plugin only tracks hemp picked up through the OnCollectiblePickup event, which applies to wild hemp, not hemp grown in planters.

---

 3. How many points per plant?

Each hemp plant gives 1 point.

---

 4. How is Hemp Gather different from Corn Collector?

Both events share the same mechanics (collecting resources), but they track different plants: Hemp Gather tracks hemp, Corn Collector tracks corn. This lets you run varied events on the same server.

---

 5. Can I run Hemp Gather and Corn Collector at the same time?

Technically yes, but it might confuse players. Better to run them one at a time through MultiEvents scheduling.

---

 Troubleshooting

 Points aren't awarded

Symptoms: A player collects plants but points don't register.

Cause: The event isn't active or the player is collecting something other than hemp.

Fix: Make sure the event is running with `hempgather.start`. Verify the player is collecting hemp, not other plants.

---

 Not enough hemp on the map

Symptoms: Players can't find enough hemp.

Cause: The map doesn't have enough hemp or resources haven't respawned.

Fix: Wait for resources to respawn. Hemp grows mainly in forest and grassland biomes. Consider increasing the event duration.

---

 Event doesn't register in MultiEvents

Symptoms: Hemp Gather doesn't appear in the MultiEvents event list.

Cause: MultiEvents loaded after Hemp Gather.

Fix: Reload the plugin: `oxide.reload MultiEventsHempGather`.

---

 Event panel doesn't show

Symptoms: The event started but the UI panel isn't visible.

Cause: ImageLibrary issue or the player hid the panel.

Fix: Make sure ImageLibrary is installed. The panel will reappear on the next event start.

FAQ and Troubleshooting

 Common Questions

 1. Which animals count?

By default: chickens (1 point), zombies (2 points), wolves, boars, horses, and stags (4 points), bears, polar bears, tigers, crocodiles, panthers, snakes, and deer (10 points). The full list is in the config and can be customized.

---

 2. How do I add a new animal?

Open the config and add a new entry to `"Score Table (animal - score)"`:

"Score Table (animal - score)": {
 "chicken": 1.0,
 "new_animal": 5.0
}

Use `me.hunt.animals.list` from the server console to get ShortPrefabName values.

---

 3. Do NPC bot kills count?

No. The plugin only tracks deaths of entities whose ShortPrefabName is in the point table. By default, that's only animals.

---

 4. Does the kill method matter?

No. Any kill method (weapons, traps, drowning, etc.) counts as long as the attacker is a player.

---

 5. What if there aren't many animals on the map?

Increase the event duration or wait for animals to respawn. You can also lower point values for rare animals to keep the event balanced.

---

 6. Can I set different points for the same animal at different levels?

No. Each animal has a fixed point value defined in the config table.

---

 Troubleshooting

 Points aren't awarded for killing animals

Symptoms: A player kills animals but points don't register.

Cause: The killed animal type isn't in the point table, or the event isn't active.

Fix: Check the animal's ShortPrefabName and make sure it's in the `"Score Table (animal - score)"` section. Verify the event is running.

---

 `me.hunt.animals.list` command doesn't work

Symptoms: The command doesn't output anything.

Cause: The command only works from the server (RCON) console.

Fix: Run the command from the server console, not from in-game chat.

---

 Event doesn't register in MultiEvents

Symptoms: Hunt Animal doesn't appear in the event list.

Cause: MultiEvents wasn't loaded or loaded after Hunt Animal.

Frequently Asked Questions

 General

 How does scoring work?

Every second, the system checks the vertical position (height) of each active player on the server. If a player's current height exceeds their previous max, their record updates. Only heights below the config limit (`Max Height`) count.

 Do players need to sign up for the event?

No. All players on the server automatically participate. Just be at a high point on the map -- a mountain top, a building roof, a tower, whatever works.

 What happens if a player goes above the max height?

Heights above the `Max Height` config value won't be recorded. This prevents abuse from flying vehicles or cheats.

 Configuration

 Can I set rewards for more than 2 positions?

Yes. Add more entries in the `Player Awards (top - award)` config section. The key is the position number (3, 4, 5, etc.).

 How do I change the event duration?

Change the `Event Duration` config value. It's in seconds (default: 600 = 10 minutes).

 Can I use commands as rewards?

Yes. Set the reward type to `Command` and specify the console command. Use `%steamid%` and `%username%` as placeholders for player data.

 Technical

 Is the plugin compatible with Carbon?

Yes. It works with both Oxide and Carbon.

 Can I run this mode at the same time as other Multi Events modes?

Event scheduling is managed by the core Multi Events plugin. Check its documentation for configuring schedules and concurrent events.

King Of Hill

 What does this mode do?

King Of Hill is an event mode for Multi Events where players compete to reach the highest point on the map. The system tracks each player's maximum height throughout the event and updates the leaderboard in real time. When time runs out, players with the highest recorded positions get rewards.

 Key Features

- Automatic tracking of each player's max height reached
- Real-time leaderboard with UI notifications
- Configurable max allowed height (to filter out cheaters)
- Flexible reward system with items, commands, kits, and plugin calls
- Integration with Multi Events Inventory and Multi Events Leaderboard
- Notify / UINotify support
- Works with both Oxide and Carbon

 Requirements

- Multi Events (core plugin) -- required
- Image Library -- recommended (for icon display)
- Notify or UINotify -- optional (for pop-up notifications)
- Multi Events Inventory -- optional (for event inventory rewards)
- Multi Events Leaderboard -- optional (for global stats)

 Quick Start

1. Install Multi Events (if you haven't already)
2. Place the mode file in `oxide/plugins` (or `carbon/plugins`)
3. Restart the server or load the plugin via console
4. The mode auto-registers with Multi Events
5. Configure as needed (see Configuration)

 Step-by-Step Installation

 Step 1. Download the Plugin File

Download the mode file and place it in your server's plugin folder:

- Oxide: `oxide/plugins/`
- Carbon: `carbon/plugins/`

 Step 2. Load the Plugin

Restart the server or load the plugin via the server console. The config file is created automatically on first load.

 Step 3. Configure

The config file is created automatically on first launch. See Configuration for details.

 Step 4. Test It

Run `kingofhill.start` in the server console to manually start the event and verify everything works.

 Updating

When updating, just replace the file in the plugins folder. The config updates automatically and keeps your settings.

 Load Order

The plugin auto-registers with Multi Events on load. If Multi Events loads later, registration happens automatically.

Commands

 Console Commands

| Command | Description | Required Permission |
|---|---|---|
| `lookingloot.start` | Start the Looking Loot event manually | `multieventslookingloot.start` |
| `lookingloot.stop` | Stop the current Looking Loot event | `multieventslookingloot.stop` |

 Automatic Launch

Under normal operation, the event starts and stops automatically through the core Multi Events plugin. Manual commands are for admins and testing.

 Notes

- Commands work from both the server console and the in-game console (F1) with the right permission
- If the event is already running, a second `lookingloot.start` is ignored
- The `lookingloot.stop` command immediately ends the event and gives rewards to current leaders

Configuration

The config file is created automatically on first plugin load.

 Main Settings

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Work with Notify? | bool | `true` | Use Notify/UINotify for notifications |
| Default Score | float | `1` | Default point multiplier for items not in the table |
| Event Duration | int | `600` | Event duration in seconds |

 Item Value Table

The `Score Table (shortname - score)` section lets you set custom point multipliers for specific items.

"Score Table (shortname - score)": {
 "scrap": 2.0,
 "gears": 2.0
}

- Key -- item short name
- Value -- point multiplier for that item

Items not in the table use the `Default Score` value.

 Award Settings

The `Awards Settings` section configures winner rewards.

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Put the reward in the MultiEventsInventory? | bool | `false` | Deliver rewards through Multi Events Inventory |
| Player Awards (top - award) | object | see below | Reward config by ranking position |

 Reward Config by Position

Rewards are configured per ranking position. The key is the position number (1, 2, 3, etc.):

"Player Awards (top - award)": {
 "1": {
 "Award IDs from MultiEventsInventory": [0, 1, 2, 3],
 "Amount": { "Minimal": 1, "Maximum": 1 },
 "Awards": [...]
 }
}

 Reward Types

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| Type | Reward type: `Item`, `Command`, `Plugin`, `Kit` |
| Chance | Drop chance from 1 to 100 |
| Item | Item settings (ShortName, Amount Min, Amount Max, Skin, DisplayName) |
| Command (%steamid%) | Console command (supports `%steamid%` and `%username%`) |
| Kit | Kit name to give |
| Plugin | Third-party plugin settings (Hook, Plugin Name, Amount) |

 Default Config Example

{
 "Work with Notify?": true,
 "Default Score": 1.0,
 "Event Duration": 600,
 "Score Table (shortname - score)": {
 "scrap": 2.0,
 "gears": 2.0
 },
 "Awards Settings": {
 "Put the reward in the MultiEventsInventory?": false,
 "Player Awards

Frequently Asked Questions

 General

 How does scoring work?

Every time a player picks up an item from a container, they earn points. The amount depends on the item value table in the config. Items not in the table use the default multiplier (`Default Score`).

 Do players need to register to participate?

No. All players on the server automatically participate. Just loot containers during the event.

 Which containers count?

All standard map containers count -- crates, barrels, loot containers at monuments, and so on.

 Configuration

 How do I make certain items worth more?

Add the item and its multiplier to the `Score Table (shortname - score)` config section. For example, to make `scrap` worth 5x the points, set its value to `5.0`.

 Can I disable scoring for certain items?

Yes. Add the item to the value table with a multiplier of `0`.

 How do I change the event duration?

Change the `Event Duration` config value. It's in seconds (default: 600 = 10 minutes).

 Technical

 Is the plugin compatible with Carbon?

Yes. It works with both Oxide and Carbon.

 Can I set rewards for more than 2 positions?

Yes. Add more entries in the `Player Awards (top - award)` config section with the position numbers you want.

Looking Loot

 What does this mode do?

Looking Loot is an event mode for Multi Events where players compete to find and loot items from containers across the map. Each item you grab earns points, and different items can have different values. The player with the most points when time runs out wins.

 Key Features

- Automatic point tracking for every looted item
- Configurable item value table (each item can have its own point multiplier)
- Default point multiplier for items not in the table
- Real-time leaderboard
- Flexible reward system by ranking position
- Integration with Multi Events Inventory and Multi Events Leaderboard
- Notify / UINotify support
- Works with both Oxide and Carbon

 Requirements

- Multi Events (core plugin) -- required
- Image Library -- recommended (for icon display)
- Notify or UINotify -- optional (for pop-up notifications)
- Multi Events Inventory -- optional (for event inventory rewards)
- Multi Events Leaderboard -- optional (for global stats)

2. Place the mode file in `oxide/plugins` (or `carbon/plugins`)
3. Restart the server or load the plugin via console
4. The mode auto-registers with Multi Events
5. Configure the item value table and rewards (see Configuration)

2. Competition -- players search for and loot items from crates, barrels, and other containers across the map. Each item earns points based

Frequently Asked Questions

 General Questions

 Is Multi Events required for this mode to work?

Yes, Multi Events is a required dependency. Mushroom Madness is an expansion mode that cannot work independently without the core plugin.

 Which mushrooms are counted?

All mushrooms on the map are counted (objects containing "mushroom" in the prefab name). These are standard Rust collectible mushrooms.

 How long does the event last?

By default, the event lasts 10 minutes (600 seconds). The duration can be changed in the configuration via the `Event Duration` parameter.

 Configuration

 How do I configure rewards?

In the `Awards Settings` section of the configuration, you can set rewards for each placement. Items, console commands, Kit sets, and third-party plugin calls are supported.

 Can I add more prize placements?

Yes. In the `Awards Settings` section, you can add additional entries with placement numbers (3, 4, 5, etc.), each with its own set of rewards.

 How do I change the event duration?

Edit the `Event Duration` parameter in the configuration. The value is specified in seconds. Reload the plugin after making changes.

 Problems

 Points are not awarded for collecting mushrooms

Make sure the event is actually running and mushrooms are present on the map. Mushrooms are standard collectible objects on the Rust map.

 The UI widget is not displayed

Check that the ImageLibrary plugin is installed and working correctly. Also make sure the player has not hidden the widget manually.

 The event ends but rewards are not given

Make sure the reward settings in the configuration are filled in correctly: correct item ShortNames, amounts, and drop chances are specified.

Mushroom Madness

 What does this mode do?

Mushroom Madness is an event mode for Multi Events where players compete for points by collecting mushrooms across the map. Points are awarded for each mushroom collected. The player with the most points when the timer expires receives rewards.

 Key Features

- Automatic point tracking for mushroom collection
- Flexible reward system for multiple leaderboard positions
- Notification support through Notify and UINotify
- Built-in UI widget with leaderboard and countdown
- Reward support through items, commands, kits (Kit), and third-party plugins
- Integration with the event inventory (Multi Events Inventory)

Frequently Asked Questions

 General

 Do I need Multi Events for this mode to work?

Yes. Multi Events is a required dependency. Npc Underwater Lab is an expansion mode that can't run on its own without the core plugin.

 Which NPCs count?

Only underwater dwellers (UnderwaterDweller) inside underwater labs count -- specifically entities named "npc_underwaterdweller". Killing NPCs in other locations won't earn points.

 How long does the event last?

10 minutes (600 seconds) by default. You can change this with the `Event Duration` setting in the config.

 Configuration

 How do I set up rewards?

In the `Awards Settings` section of the config, define rewards for each position. Items, console commands, Kits, and third-party plugin calls are all supported.

 Can I add more prize positions?

Yes. Add extra entries with position numbers (3, 4, 5, etc.) to the `Awards Settings` section, each with its own set of rewards.

 How do I change the event duration?

Edit the `Event Duration` parameter in the config. The value is in seconds. Reload the plugin after making changes.

 Problems

 Points aren't awarded for killing NPCs

Make sure the event is actually running and you're killing NPCs inside underwater labs specifically. NPCs at other monuments don't count for this mode.

 The map doesn't have underwater labs

This mode requires underwater labs on the map. Make sure your map includes these monuments.

 The UI widget doesn't show up

Check that ImageLibrary is installed and working. Also make sure the player hasn't manually hidden the widget.

 The event ends but rewards aren't given out

Make sure the reward settings in your config are filled in correctly: valid item ShortNames, amounts, and drop chances.

Npc Underwater Lab

 What does this mode do?

Npc Underwater Lab is an event mode for Multi Events where players compete by killing underwater dwellers (NPCs) in underwater labs. Every NPC kill earns points. The player with the highest score when time runs out wins the rewards.

 Key Features

- Automatic point tracking for underwater NPC kills (UnderwaterDweller)
- Flexible reward system for multiple leaderboard positions
- Notify and UINotify notification support
- Built-in UI widget with leaderboard and countdown
- Rewards via items, commands, Kits, and third-party plugins
- Integration with the event inventory (Multi Events Inventory)

Frequently Asked Questions

 General

 Do I need Multi Events for this mode to work?

Yes. Multi Events is a required dependency. Patrol Helicopter Hit is an expansion mode that can't run on its own without the core plugin.

 How does scoring work?

Every hit on the event helicopter earns one point. All hits count, regardless of what weapon you're using.

 What happens when the helicopter is destroyed?

If the helicopter goes down before the timer expires, the event ends automatically. Winners are determined by total hits scored.

 How long does the event last?

10 minutes (600 seconds) by default. The event can also end early if the helicopter is destroyed. You can change the duration with the `Event Duration` setting.

 Configuration

 How do I change the helicopter's health?

Edit the `Helicopter Health` parameter in the config. Higher values mean the helicopter survives longer, giving players more time to score hits.

 How do I set the helicopter spawn position?

Set the coordinates in the `Heli Init Position` parameter. These correspond to in-game map coordinates. Altitude is set separately with `Heli Height above ground`.

 How do I set up rewards?

In the `Awards Settings` section of the config, define rewards for each position. Items, console commands, Kits, and third-party plugin calls are all supported.

 Can I add more prize positions?

Yes. Add extra entries with position numbers (3, 4, 5, etc.) to the `Awards Settings` section, each with its own set of rewards.

 Problems

 The helicopter doesn't spawn when the event starts

Check the position settings in the config. Make sure the coordinates are within the map boundaries.

 Points aren't awarded for hits

Make sure the event is running and you're shooting the event helicopter specifically. Hits on other helicopters (not spawned by the plugin) don't count.

 The UI widget doesn't show up

Check that ImageLibrary is installed and working. Also make sure the player hasn't manually hidden the widget.
